leadText: Bite (Japanese: かみつく Bite) is a damage-dealing Dark-type move introduced in Generation I. Prior to Generation II, it was a Normal-type move.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generation II onward 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] Bite is a Normal-type move in this generation. Bite inflicts damage and has a 10% chance of causing the target to flinch . Generation II onward [ edit | edit source ] Bite is now a Dark-type move and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch. Due to the change in type it is now a special move in Generations II and III before reverting to a physical move starting in Generation IV. Bite can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 1 bonus appeal point if Leer or Scary Face was used in the previous turn. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Stad2 A bite made using sharp fangs. This may cause the opponent to flinch, and it might not attack. G S C An attack that may cause flinching. R S E Colo XD Bites with vicious fangs. May cause flinching. FR LG The user bites with vicious fangs. It may make the foe flinch. D P Pt HG SS PBR The foe is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. It may make the target flinch. B W B2 W2 The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. It may make the target flinch. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP S V The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. This may also make the target flinch. LA The user bites the target with viciously sharp fangs to inflict damage. ZA The user attacks by biting targets with viciously sharp fangs. This may also make targets flinch.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Inflicts damage on the target. It may also cause the target to cringe, making it incapable of action. MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target. It may also afflict the targeted enemy with a Cringe status, making its actions fail. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる しかも いっていのかくりつで ひるみじょうたいにして わざや こうげきを だせなくする Conq. The target is bitten with viciously sharp fangs. It may make the target flinch. MD GtI It damages an enemy. It could also cause the Cringe status condition, which makes the enemy unable to attack or use moves. S MD MD RT DX It damages an enemy. It could also make the enemy flinch.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Bite is one of the four moves to have its type changed in Generation II , the others being Gust , Karate Chop , and Sand Attack . They were all Normal-type moves in Generation I . Bite is the only of these moves that changed to a type introduced in Generation II. Bite is the only move to have its damage category change twice. This is due to the move being Normal-type (and therefore physical) in Generation I , Dark-type (and therefore special) in Generation II and III , and a physical move from Generation IV onward. If Secret Power is used in a cave in Generation III, it will have the same animation as Bite.
